commit.message:
feat: add Transfusion and Emergency feature modules (M5-030, M5-038) (#37)

* feat(transfusion): add complete Transfusion feature module

- Add domain models with enums for CrossMatch, BloodIssue, Transfusion,
  TransfusionReaction, and HemovigilanceReport
- Add TransfusionService with firstValueFrom HTTP calls for all API endpoints
- Add CrossMatchRequestComponent: form to submit cross-match requests + table view
- Add CrossMatchResultComponent: filterable request list with result detail panel
- Add BloodIssueComponent: issue blood against compatible requests + recent issues table
- Add TransfusionRecordComponent: tabbed start/complete forms + recent transfusions table
- Add ReactionReportComponent: reaction form with critical reaction warning banner
- Add HemovigilanceListComponent: paginated report list with status filter
- Add TRANSFUSION_ROUTES with roleGuard (DOCTOR, NURSE) on every route
- Register transfusion routes under staff portal in app.routes.ts

All components: standalone, OnPush, inject(), signal(), @if/@for, firstValueFrom

Co-authored-by: rasterwebapps <256557304+rasterwebapps@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(emergency): add complete Emergency feature module

- models/emergency.model.ts: EmergencyRequest, EmergencyMatch, DisasterEvent,
  DonorMobilization with enums, create payloads, and select option constants
- services/emergency.service.ts: listEmergencies, getEmergency, createEmergency,
  getMatches, listDisasters, getDisaster, createDisaster via firstValueFrom
- emergency-request component: ReactiveForm with all required fields (patientName,
  patientId, bloodGroup, rhFactor, componentType, unitsRequired, priorityLevel,
  hospitalName, notes), warn/primary button based on priority, recent requests table
- emergency-dashboard component: summary cards (active/matched/dispatched counts),
  active emergencies table with priority badges, 30s auto-refresh via setInterval/
  clearInterval, OnInit/OnDestroy lifecycle
- disaster-response component: create disaster form + disaster list with detail panel
  (selectedDisaster signal), restricted to SUPER_ADMIN and REGIONAL_ADMIN
- emergency.routes.ts: lazy-loaded routes with roleGuard on every route
- app.routes.ts: register /staff/emergency under staff portal

commit.message:
fix: downgrade permanent redirects (301→302, 308→307) to prevent browser caching

Browsers cache 301 Moved Permanently and 308 Permanent Redirect
responses indefinitely at the OS/browser level.  The Location URLs
emitted by maskproxy contain proxy-internal paths (/__sd__/…) and
localhost addresses that are session-specific.  If cached and the proxy
is restarted, switched to a different target, or the user opens a fresh
browser tab, those stale cached redirects cause redirect loops or point
to the wrong host.

Fix: in modifyResponse, downgrade 301→302 and 308→307 so the browser
always re-asks the proxy on the next visit instead of serving the
redirect from its internal cache.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add Azure DevOps extension documentation

Replace the 'Coming Soon' placeholder with full documentation for the

commit.message:
feat: Deep Agentic integration, Notepad app, and Proactive AURA intelligence

🤖 Deep Agentic Capabilities:
  • Notepad application with localStorage, cloud upload, and real file downloads
  • AURA voice commands: 'Create a file', 'Write essay about [topic]', 'Download file'
  • Voice-controlled text generation and injection into Notepad

⚡ Edge AI Optimization:
  • Fast-path local regex parser for 'open notepad' (<50ms latency)
  • Zero LLM calls for app launches - instant execution
  • Only LLM for content generation tasks

🎯 Proactive Intelligence:
  • Monitor window count and trigger proactive alerts when >10 windows open
  • AURA speaks: 'Sir, the system feels a bit heavy. Shall I close background apps?'
  • Elegant UI notification with 4-second timeout
  • Auto-hide if no user response within timeout window
  • 30-second cooldown between alerts to prevent spam

📝 Features Implemented:
  • artifacts/kali-portfolio/src/components/NotepadApp.tsx - Classic Notepad UI
  • artifacts/kali-portfolio/src/lib/ProactiveMonitor.ts - Window monitoring & alerts
  • artifacts/kali-portfolio/src/components/ProactiveNotification.tsx - Alert UI
  • Updated Desktop.tsx - Notepad integration + monitor initialization
  • Updated AuraService.ts - Notepad in voice command mapper

✨ Zero Breaking Changes:
  • No new dependencies
  • Surgical code additions (~400 lines)
  • Follows existing MONIX OS patterns
  • Integrated with existing AURA pipeline

commit.message:
fix: correctly handle ccTLD targets in computeRootDomain

When the target is a 3-label ccTLD domain like ynet.co.il, bbc.co.uk,
or domain.com.au, the previous heuristic stripped the first label and
returned co.il / co.uk / com.au as the "root domain".  This caused the
subdomain masking regex to match ALL *.co.il domains (not just
*.ynet.co.il), routing unrelated external sites (analytics, CDNs,
third-party trackers) through the /__sd__/ mechanism.  Those external
sites had their own redirect chains which, when processed through the
proxy with user string replacements, created redirect loops that
triggered ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS in Chrome.

Fix: add a knownSLDs lookup table of well-known second-level domain
labels (co, com, net, org, gov, ac, edu, …).  When stripping the leading
label from a 3-label host would produce a 2-label co.XX / com.XX style
suffix, the original hostname is already the registrable domain and is
returned unchanged.

Examples:
  ynet.co.il  → ynet.co.il  (was: co.il)
  bbc.co.uk   → bbc.co.uk   (was: co.uk)
  domain.com.au → domain.com.au (was: com.au)
  www.ynet.co.il → ynet.co.il  (unchanged — 4 labels, strips www)

Also fix the startup log to always print the subdomain masking scope,
including when rootDomain == targetHost (e.g. ynet.co.il, github.com).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add rollup linux binaries as explicit optionalDependencies

Regenerates package-lock.json so @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu and
@rollup/rollup-linux-x64-musl are included and installed on Railway.

commit.message:
feat: AdaptiveConcurrency の増速制御を成功カウントから時間ベース（秒数）に変更 (#133)

* feat: AdaptiveConcurrency の増速制御を成功カウントから時間ベースに変更

- SuccessThreshold（成功カウント）→ IncreaseIntervalSec（秒数）に変更
- 小ファイル多数移行時の過敏な増速を防止
- Retry-After は加算方式: now + retryAfter + increaseIntervalSec
- デフォルト 30 秒、0 指定で即時増速可能（テスト向け）
- SetIncreaseAvailableNow() 内部メソッドをテスト補助用に追加
- InitialDegree（ソフトスタート）機能含む（SettingsPage UI 対応済み）

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: PRレビュー指摘対応（AdaptiveConcurrency 時間ベース制御の修正）

- AdaptiveConcurrencyController: NotifyRateLimit で DecreaseTriggerCount>1 の場合も
  pendingDecreases 加算時点で _increaseAvailableAfterTicks を更新し、減速発火前の
  即時増速を防止
- AdaptiveConcurrencyController: NotifySuccess の XML コメントを IncreaseIntervalSec
  ベースの説明に更新（旧 SuccessThreshold 記述を修正）
- SharePointMigrationPipeline: Phase C の MaxDegreeOfParallelism を
  min(MaxParallelFolderCreations, controller.MaxDegree) でキャップし、
  MaxParallelFolderCreations 設定が無視されるバグを修正
- ConfigurationService: adaptiveConcurrency.sharepoint キーが存在しない場合に
  default プロファイルへフォールバックし、設定保存で enabled=false が書き込まれて
  アダプティブ制御が無効化されるバグを修正
- SettingsPage: AdaptiveConcurrencyInitialDegree の 0〜256 範囲バリデーションを追加
- AdaptiveConcurrencyControllerTests: コメント誤字「合記」→「合計」を修正

Co-Authored-By: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: PRレビュー第2回指摘対応（AdaptiveConcurrency / Phase C）

- AdaptiveConcurrencyController: _increaseAvailableAfterTicks の更新を
  if (_current > _min) ガード外に移動し、MinDegree 張り付き時にも
  429/503 直後の NotifySuccess による即時増速を防止
- SharePointMigrationPipeline: Phase C の current_parallelism 記録を
  Math.Min(maxDegree, controller?.CurrentDegree ?? maxDegree) に統一し、
  MaxParallelFolderCreations キャップを超えた値がダッシュボードに表示されないよう修正
- AdaptiveConcurrencyControllerTests: ソフトスタートテストのコメント「SetIncreaseAvailableNow 後に」を削除
  （increaseIntervalSec=0 のため不要、実装と不整合だった）

commit.message:
fix: channel-based exfil logger — eliminate concurrent write race

Replace shared *os.File with a buffered channel (cap 256) owned by a
single writer goroutine:

- Multiple handleConn goroutines call writeExfilLog concurrently;
  concurrent WriteString on the same *os.File is not safe and could
  produce interleaved or lost log entries.

- The writer goroutine is the sole owner of the file handle; it reads
  from exfilCh and writes entries sequentially, surfacing any write
  errors to stderr rather than silently discarding them.

- On SIGTERM/SIGINT the accept loop exits, exfilCh is closed, and
  writerDone.Wait() ensures all queued detection events are flushed
  before the process exits.

- writeExfilLog uses a non-blocking select/default so a full channel
  (e.g. during an unexpected burst) logs a warning to stderr rather
  than blocking the ICAP handler goroutine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add verbose logging and log-file support

New flags:
  -verbose    log every request/response with full headers + body preview
              (4 KiB cap, sensitive headers redacted: Authorization, Cookie, etc.)
  -log <path> tee all output to a file (append mode) in addition to stderr

Default mode now logs one line per request/response:
  -> GET    https://upstream/path
  <- 200    https://upstream/path  (14.2 KB, 42ms)

New file: logger.go — Logger struct wrapping log.Logger with:
  Printf / Fatal (always shown)
  Verbosef (verbose-only)
  LogRequest(req, bodySnapshot) time.Time
  LogResponse(resp, bodySnapshot, start)
  bodyDump, sortedKeys, humanBytes helpers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use local date strings to prevent UTC timezone shift in event matching

Melbourne (UTC+10/+11) midnight converts to previous day in UTC when
using toISOString().slice(0,10). Use local year/month/day formatting
instead to ensure date comparisons match the user's selected date.

commit.message:
v0.2.66: Auto-purge stale overdue instances on new cycle

When generate_instances creates a new instance for a chore, any
pending/overdue instances from previous cycles (due_date < today)
are deleted first. Keeps the schedule clean without accumulation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: HA-chores
repository.description: Gamified household chore management for Home Assistant

commit.message:
fix: repair docs site build and deployment workflow

- Fix Starlight social config: array → object format (v0.32+ breaking change)
- Add src/content.config.ts for Astro 5 content layer API (required by docsLoader)
- Pin astro@5.7.5 and @astrojs/starlight@0.32.2 for version stability
- Override @astrojs/sitemap to 3.7.0 to avoid Zod v3/v4 conflict
- Update deploy workflow: Node 22, cancel-in-progress, github.pages_url
- Add package-lock.json (required by npm ci in CI)
- Add .gitignore for node_modules, dist, .astro

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve ruff-format issues in state_converter and deploy_agent

- Collapse multi-line expressions that fit within line length limit
- Move noqa: S607 comment to the correct line after reformatting

repository.name: QPrisma
repository.description: QPrisma is an enterprise-grade, AI-powered multimedia processing platform designed to unlock insights from video and image content. By combining computer vision, Large Language Models (LLMs), and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), QPrisma transforms unstructured media into searchable, actionable knowledge.
